objects.name: Analytic 1678
objects.description: From the defender’s view: a sandboxed app retrieves code-like content (JS/Mach-O/bundles), writes it to container tmp/Caches, performs memory permission changes (RW→RX/RWX) or directly loads via dyld/dlopen from writable paths, sometimes preceded by 3rd-party hotpatch frameworks (e.g., JSPatch-like behavior) or script engine evaluation. The analytic correlates Network Content → File Creation → OS API Execution (memory permission change) → Module Load (dyld/dlopen) and/or Process Access (codesign validation touches), with optional scripting engine events.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: iOS:unifiedlog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Per-App VPN flow with code-like content types (application/octet-stream, application/zip, text/javascript, application/x-mach-o)

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: iOS:unifiedlog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Create/write in /var/mobile/Containers/Data/Application/<GUID>/(tmp|Library/Caches)/ for .js/.bundle/.dylib/.zip with elevated entropy

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: iOS:unifiedlog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: mmap/mprotect transitions to PROT_EXEC for pages associated with recently written files

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: iOS:unifiedlog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: dlopen/image load from app-writable path (tmp, Caches) outside bundled resources

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Max correlation window between download → write → load (e.g., 15–60s).
MIME list treated as code-like (octet-stream, zip, javascript, x-mach-o).
Regex for app container tmp/Caches writable paths.
Entropy cutoff to flag code blobs (e.g., ≥ 7.3).
Bundles that legitimately do JIT/script eval to reduce RWX noise.
Regex for loads from writable paths only (exclude app bundle).
Foreground/background or Work Profile state to filter noise.
